title: Unnecessary Bit Operations Should Not Be Performed
description: Certain bit operations, though syntactically correct, do not add any functional value to the code as their results are always predictable. For instance, operations like 'using & -1' with any value will always result in the original value. The same is true for 'anyValue ^ 0' and 'anyValue | 0'. They should be avoided for cleaner, more efficient code.
recommendation: Refactor the code to remove unnecessary bit operations like '& -1', '^ 0', and '| 0'. These operations do not add any functional value and their results are always predictable.
impact: Performing unnecessary operations can decrease the efficiency of the code and make it more complex. It can also lead to confusion and potential errors in future code modifications.

checks.message: Refactor the code to remove unnecessary bit operations like '& -1', '^ 0', and '| 0'. These operations do not add any functional value and their results are always predictable.
